questionHtml: Explain, with the help of equations, the mechanism of the free-radical substitution reaction of ethane with bromine in presence of sunlight.
markschemeHtml:
Initiation:
Br2 2Br•

Propagation:
Br• + C2H6 → C2H5• + HBr

C2H5• + Br2 → C2H5Br + Br•

Termination:
Br• + Br• → Br2

OR

C2H5• + Br• → C2H5Br

OR

C2H5• + C2H5• → C4H10

Reference to UV/hν/heat not required.

Accept representation of radical without • (eg, Br, C2H5) if consistent throughout mechanism.

Accept further bromination.

Award [3 max] if initiation, propagation and termination are not stated or are incorrectly labelled for equations.

Award [3 max] if methane is used instead of ethane, and/or chlorine is used instead of bromine.

meta.title: Explain, with the help of equations, the mechanism of the free-radical substitution reaction of ethane with bromine in presence of sunlight.
